query: SK Hynix ADR Nasdaq listing SKHY first trading date July 2026 latest news, pricing date, roadshow status, SEC review as of late June 2026. Is July 10 still the expected trading date? Any reports of delay or schedule slippage?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

**Bottom line:** As of June 30, 2026, **July 10, 2026 remains the expected first trading date** for SK Hynix's ADSs on Nasdaq (ticker **SKHY**). **No delay or schedule slippage has been reported** — the timeline is actually *ahead* of the earlier August expectation.

**Key status points:**
- **F-1/A amendment filed Monday, June 30, 2026** (original F-1 June 24; confidential filing March 24/25). The SEC registration had **not yet become effective** as of June 30 — all dates remain explicitly "tentative and subject to change."
- **Roadshow:** Non-deal roadshows with overseas institutions were completed in early June; a formal global roadshow (US/Europe/Asia) was planned post-effectiveness ahead of demand-forecast pricing.
- **Offering:** Up to 17.79M new ADSs (~2.5% of shares), targeting ~45.45T KRW (~$29.4–29.65B) — the largest ADR offering ever. Indicative ADS price ~$166; underwriters BofA, Citi, Goldman, JPMorgan.
- **Other dates:** Subscription/payment July 14; domestic new-share listing July 29 (same day as Q2 earnings).
- **Risk context (not delays):** A DRAM price-fixing class action was filed June 25, and late-June KOSPI volatility was extreme (June 23 9.99% drop/circuit breaker; SK Hynix −8.4% June 26).

The full structured findings (key facts, 14 sources, and 12 quantitative data points) have been recorded.

key_facts: July 10, 2026 remains the scheduled first trading date for SK Hynix ADSs on Nasdaq (ticker SKHY) as of June 30, 2026 — explicitly tentative and subject to SEC review.
No reports of delay or schedule slippage; the July 10 target is actually AHEAD of the earlier (June) expectation of an August debut.
SK Hynix filed an amended registration statement (Form F-1/A) with the SEC on Monday, June 30, 2026; the registration had NOT yet become effective as of that date.
Public Form F-1 was filed June 24, 2026; confidential SEC filing was made March 24/25, 2026.

Offering: up to 17.79 million new ADSs (~2.5% of ~712.7M outstanding shares), targeting up to 45.45 trillion KRW (~$29.4–29.65 billion) — would be the largest ADR offering ever, surpassing Alibaba's $21.8B (2014).
Other scheduled dates: subscription/payment July 14, 2026; domestic new-share listing July 29, 2026 (same day as Q2 earnings).
Non-deal roadshows with overseas institutions completed in early June 2026; a formal global roadshow (US/Europe/Asia) planned after registration effectiveness, ahead of demand-forecast pricing.
Underwriters/global coordinators: BofA Securities, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, J.P. Morgan.
Indicative ADS price ~$166; HSBC assigned a ~20% premium to the ADR listing price (P/B 2.8x to 3.4x); Mirae Asset projects ~$1.5B in passive ETF inflows.
Board approved the offering June 24, 2026 (all six outside directors present); each common share is represented by 10 ADRs.
Earlier Reuters reporting (June 10) indicated SEC clearance could arrive week of June 22, and Korea's FSS document review could finish as early as July 3, 2026.
Risk context (not delays): DRAM price-fixing class action filed June 25, 2026; extreme late-June KOSPI volatility including a June 23 9.99% drop/circuit breaker and SK Hynix -8.4% on June 26.
SK Hynix shares (000660.KS) closed at 2,650,000 KRW on June 30, 2026, ~11% below the 52-week high of 2,987,000 KRW (June 22); up ~300% in 2026.

data_points.metric: Scheduled Nasdaq ADS first trading date
Offering as share of outstanding stock
Projected US ETF inflows post-listing (Mirae Asset)
